audit_metadata.auditor: Claude Code Supply Chain Security Analysis
audit_metadata.scope: 18 direct production dependencies, 8 dev dependencies, ~1544 transitive packages
audit_metadata.methodology: OWASP Supply Chain Security + npm package behavioral analysis

key_risks: @solana/wallet-adapter-wallets pulls in 623+ transitive deps including deprecated WalletConnect packages — largest attack surface
GSAP has proprietary license with commercial restrictions — needs legal review
@anthropic-ai/sdk is pre-1.0 with caret range — should pin to exact version
tweetnacl unmaintained since 2019 — should pin to exact 1.0.3 to prevent accepting compromised future patch

install_scripts.details: blake-hash, bufferutil, protobufjs, sharp, tiny-secp256k1, unrs-resolver, usb, utf-8-validate — all well-known native addons

obfuscated_code.eval_usage: 6 files — all in eslint-scope, protobufjs, react-devtools-core, terser (legitimate)
obfuscated_code.function_constructor: 5 files — all in tapable, tmpl, source-map-js, ajv (legitimate)
obfuscated_code.base64_operations: 20 files — all in standard packages (cbor, eslint, jws, postcss, etc.)

network_calls.details: No suspicious hardcoded URLs or IP addresses. All network references in well-known packages.

process_execution.details: 110 matches inflated by regex .exec(). Actual child_process: cross-spawn, sharp, napi-postinstall, typescript.

native_binaries.details: All from @img/sharp, @next/swc, @tailwindcss/oxide, @unrs/resolver, blake-hash, bufferutil, usb, utf-8-validate

REVIEW: @anthropic-ai/sdk@^0.78.0 — pre-1.0 caret range, pin to exact
gsap@^3.14.2 — proprietary license needs legal review

recommendations.reason: Cuts 80%+ of transitive dependency tree (~623 packages → ~50)
Unmaintained since 2019, prevent accepting hypothetical compromised patch

recommendations.action: Review GSAP license or replace with framer-motion
Enable Dependabot/Renovate for automated security updates
Ensure CI uses npm ci (not npm install) for lockfile integrity
